commands.repair_reason: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the PR already contains the narrow code fix; the remaining action is ordinary review and validation.; Cleared: The diff only passes an existing boolean into an existing configured-plugin bootstrap path and does not add dependencies, scripts, permissions, workflow changes, or secret handling. (sha=33c519073fc347ddf615c0befc0b6fecc3250544)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes message channel selection to pass `allowBootstrap: true` when validating explicit or fallback outbound channels.

Reproducibility: yes. this is source-reproducible: current main's message-tool channel selection calls the resolver without `allowBootstrap`, while the resolver only bootstraps configured but unloaded channel adapters when that flag is true. I did not run a full live `openclaw agent --local` E2E reproduction.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the PR already contains the narrow code fix; the remaining action is ordinary review and validation.

target.title: fix(channels): pass allowBootstrap from channel-selection so in-agent message tool resolves channels in --local processes
target.body:
## Problem

The in-agent `openclaw_message` tool intermittently throws "Channel is
unavailable: \<channel\>" against a configured channel that the CLI
send-path (`openclaw message send`) can deliver to seconds before and
after. Six occurrences observed in 14 days, all during ~04:00–05:00 UTC
heartbeat windows that route dispatches through `openclaw agent --local`.

Adjacent to #77254 (closed 2026-05-07), which fixed the cron-announce and
final-reply paths but did not touch the in-agent message tool caller.
The underlying resolver path is the same; the caller surface is different.

## Change and value

`src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.ts`'s `resolveAvailableKnownChannel`
calls `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin({channel, cfg})` — without
`allowBootstrap`. In a process where the channel plugin hasn't yet been
eagerly loaded (e.g. `openclaw agent --local`, which loads only the
hook-only plugin set at startup), the resolver returns metadata-only,
which the caller treats as "not available" and short-circuits to
`undefined`. Callers in turn surface "Channel is unavailable: \<channel\>".

Pass `allowBootstrap: true` from this caller. In gateway context, where
channels are pre-loaded eagerly, this is a no-op fast path. In
agent-local context, the existing bootstrap branch runs once to
lazy-load the channel adapter, and all subsequent message-tool calls in
that process hit the loaded plugin.

## Who's affected

Anyone running agents via `openclaw agent --local` (one-shot dispatches,
heartbeats, cron-driven workflows) whose dispatch invokes the message
tool against a configured outbound channel. The failure self-heals
asynchronously when channels load for other reasons in long-running
processes, so the fingerprint is "transient" — but for short-lived
`--local` processes that exit before incidental load, the message tool
fails outright.

## Why now

Six recurrences in 14 days; the recurring pattern is well-documented and
the call chain identified.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `openclaw agent --local` invocations
  that use the in-agent message tool no longer fail with "Channel is
  unavailable" when the channel is properly configured. Gateway-routed
  invocations are unaffected (their channel-plugin is loaded eagerly).
- **Real environment tested**: local patched build against the bundled
  `dist/channel-selection-*.js` on a stock `2026.5.19` install. Direct
  resolver probe via Node script (bypasses agent prompt-injection
  defense, which had been blocking end-to-end repro through the CLI):

  ~~~js
  // probe-channel-resolution.mjs
  import fs from "fs";
  import { r as resolveOutboundChannelPlugin } from "<dist>/channel-resolution-*.js";
  const cfg = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync("<config-path>", "utf8"));
  const before = resolveOutboundChannelPlugin({ channel: "discord", cfg });
  const after  = resolveOutboundChannelPlugin({ channel: "discord", cfg, allowBootstrap: true });
  console.log(JSON.stringify({ before, after }));
  ~~~

  Output (same process, same config, same input — only \`allowBootstrap\`
  differs):

  ~~~
  WITHOUT allowBootstrap: { resolved: false }
  WITH    allowBootstrap: { resolved: true, id: "discord", has_send_text: true }
  ~~~

- **Cross-reference**: #77254 (closed 2026-05-07; commits `3a4c8607`
  pinning the channel registry at startup and `bc9c074b` for resolver
  hardening). That fix made the cron-announce and final-reply callers
  tolerant of registry timing, but did not touch the in-agent message
  tool's caller. This change closes the remaining hole in the same
  family.
- **Tests**: no test-suite changes required for this one-line caller
  fix. The resolver's `allowBootstrap` branch is already covered by
  upstream's coverage of the cron-announce / final-reply paths.

## Implementation

One-line change: add `allowBootstrap: true` to the
`resolveOutboundChannelPlugin` call inside `resolveAvailableKnownChannel`
in `src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.ts`. No new types, no new
imports — `allowBootstrap` is already in the `params` shape of
`resolveOutboundChannelPlugin` (used by `src/infra/outbound/targets.ts`
and `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts` since #77254).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: kaspre
